methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

data.narrative:
Exotec is a Series D robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Romain Moulin, currently valued at $2.0B post-money following $335M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a universe of 9 private peers (including Divergent, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics) and 1 public comparable, spanning the robotics and manufacturing sector at the same or adjacent funding stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $3.0B from the $2.0B base. The peer-median approach references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Divergent at $2.3B (Series E), Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), and Nimble Robotics at $1.0B (Series C), producing a median implied value of $1.6B with a range of $1.0B–$2.3B. These methods reflect materially different assumptions about growth trajectory and peer comparability.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $3.4B (high), with a median of $2.3B. The wide spread reflects key uncertainties: the extent to which stage-progression multiples apply to this particular company versus broader peer performance, customer concentration and revenue visibility, and the relevance of both earlier-stage and later-stage comps to Exotec's current position. The current $2.0B valuation sits within but toward the lower end of this range.
